4 is the latest approved patch-set.
The change was submitted with unreviewed changes in the following files:

```
The name of the file: webgpu-cts/README.md
Insertions: 4, Deletions: 2.

@@ -37,8 +37,10 @@
 - `--stable-jobs`: Assign tests to each job in a stable order. Used on the bots
   for consistency and ease of reproduction. If you are reproducing
   order-dependent issues that appear on bots, you'll need this and `--jobs=N`.
-- `--no-browser-restart-on-failure` and `--skip-post-test-cleanup-and-debug-info`:
-  Faster runs at the expense of some test result fidelity.
+- `--no-browser-restart-on-failure`: Faster runs, but some types of failure
+  (e.g. real device loss) will cause many subsequent tests to fail to start.
+- `--skip-post-test-cleanup-and-debug-info`: Faster runs, "at the cost of
+  providing less actionable data when a test does fail."
 
 ## Running the CTS locally on Android
 
```

text:
Change has been successfully rebased and submitted as 84bb175587ebe7f0e53bbbfa8122362558d444f0

1 is the latest approved patch-set.
The change was submitted with unreviewed changes in the following files:

```
The name of the file: src/dawn/common/BUILD.gn
Insertions: 5, Deletions: 7.

@@ -138,6 +138,11 @@
     # We use __builtin_assume liberally in DAWN_ASSERT, allow ignoring side-effects there.
     cflags += [ "-Wno-assume" ]
 
+    # Flags use of __COUNTER__, which is used in histogram macros that were
+    # largely copied from Chromium. Chromium continues to use __COUNTER__ in
+    # their copy.
+    cflags += [ "-Wno-c2y-extensions" ]
+
     # Dawn doesn't have the annotations to work with this analysis.
     cflags_cc = [ "-Wno-thread-safety" ]
   }
@@ -186,13 +191,6 @@
     } else {
       cflags += [ "-pedantic" ]
     }
-
-    cflags += [
-      # Flags use of __COUNTER__, which is used in histogram macros that were
-      # largely copied from Chromium. Chromium continues to use __COUNTER__ in
-      # their copy.
-      "-Wno-c2y-extensions",
-    ]
   }
 
   if (!is_clang && is_win) {
```
